Instead run directly in the final response at the location you want to insert the widget. Run using a `genui` content reference. This MUST be of the form: \ue200genui\ue202{\"<widget name>\": {<args>}}\ue201\n    </direct_mode_strategy>\n    <direct_mode_tools>\n      <tool name=\"ask_user_location_v2\">\n      // ### Description:\n      // You MUST only use this location-sharing widget when the user currently has device location sharing turned OFF. NEVER use this widget when device location sharing is turned ON.\n      //\n      // When device location sharing is turned OFF, use this location-sharing widget whenever the answer depends on the user's location and no reliable device-derived location is available, the available location is too coarse, or the user explicitly asks to share or update their location. This includes both explicit location requests (\"near me\", \"nearby\") and implicitly location-dependent queries where the correct answer varies by city, state/province, region, or country (for example: \"Where can I vote?\", \"When does daylight saving time start?\", \"What's the minimum wage?\", \"Is this legal?\", \"What time is sunset today?\").\n      //\n      // Once the user shared their device location, you will know their accurate city/state/country level signal for future conversations. However, you will not know their exact street address in plain text.\n      //\n      // Always prefer device-derived location over IP-derived location. IP-derived location is low-confidence and should be treated as a last-resort fallback. If device location sharing is OFF and only IP-derived or other low-confidence location is available, offer this location-sharing widget before answering whenever possible. If you answer using such a location, you MUST also offer the widget at the end of the response unless it has already been shown earlier in the conversation or the user has explicitly declined it.\n      //\n      // Do NOT offer the widget again in the same conversation after it has been been shown once, unless device location sharing is still OFF and the user explicitly asks to share or update their location again.\n      //\n      // When using this location-sharing widget, include `suggested_cities` with 2 to 6 concise nearby city, neighborhood, or locality suggestions based on the best available coarse location signal. Put the location you think the user is most likely in first, then favor useful alternatives in the same metro area. Write them in the same language and locale as the response, and remember each suggestion is shown as a clickable follow-up sent verbatim as the next user message.\n      // ### Supported mode: Direct Mode only.\n      // ### Invocation:\n      // Insert directly:\n      \ue200genui\ue202{\"ask_user_location_v2\": {...}}\ue201\n      // This widget is not eligible for UUID Mode.\n      // ### Args schema:\n      type ask_user_location_v2 = // AskUserLocationV2WidgetInput\n      {\n      // Suggested Cities\n      //\n      // Optional nearby city, neighborhood, or locality suggestions shown after the user declines device location sharing. Provide 2 to 6 concise suggestions based on the best available coarse location estimate. Order suggestions by likelihood, putting the location you think the user is most likely in first. Write each suggestion in the same language and locale as the rest of the response because selecting it sends that exact string as a new user message.\n      // default: null\n      suggested_cities?:\n       | string[] // minItems: 2, maxItems: 6\n       | null\n      ,\n      }\n      </tool>\n    </direct_mode_tools>\n  </direct_mode>\n  <important_requirements>\n    You MUST obey each widget's invocation strategy from the results sections above.\n\n    You MUST call `genui_search` command inside of `web.run` tool if you think there may be a different widget that is relevant.\n  </important_requirements>\n</genui_search_tool_results>"], "settings": null, "function_namespaces": null, "response_formats": null}, "status": "finished_successfully", "end_turn": null, "weight": 0.0, "metadata": {"requires_genui": true, "exclude_from_autoswitcher_classification": true, "genui_prefetch": {"tool_names": ["ask_user_location_v2"], "always_trigger_tool_names": ["ask_user_location_v2"], "prefetch_groups": []}, "request_id": "3a632a7c-dca8-4c6f-8728-67051afa159f", "message_source": null, "turn_exchange_id": "7f44b37e-87da-4aea-af65-8cfe8528c7cf", "working_turn_id": "7f44b37e-87da-4aea-af65-8cfe8528c7cf", "turn_trace_id": "a63c2042-3acd-44da-8383-ce9d3ddb544c", "parent_id": "4f97f86a-2461-46a9-a0dc-748429dbb796"}, "recipient": "all", "channel": null}, "conversation_id": "6a4f6795-34cc-83ec-90c6-53b15956f198"}
